creator: Cindy Quach

evidence.text: Then you can also ask your customers, do some surveys, allowing your customers to actually reach you and leave comments and feedback. It's not great if the customer has to talk to you every time there's an outage. It is a useful data point. I've seen companies where sometimes that's their only signal, so it's not great. Other things you can do is you can use some web trend analyzers or scrapers, and you can see how many folks are mentioning your website's reliability on social media, and adjust accordingly. title: Stop Talking & Listen; Practices for Creating Effective Customer SLOs
